title: Rustic Dutch Chicken Roast
description: A hearty Dutch chicken roast packed with pickles and rosemary, baked in a single sheet for a lazy Sunday meal. The gravy is light and the chicken remains juicy and tender.
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Rinse the chicken and pat dry with paper towels; season generously with salt and pepper.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, Dijon mustard, apple cider vinegar, minced garlic and chopped rosemary.
Rub the mixture all over the chicken, ensuring the skin is coated.
Scatter chopped dill pickles, sliced lemon, onion quarters and optional almonds around the chicken on the sheet.
Pour chicken broth into the bottom of the sheet, keeping the liquid separated from the chicken.
Cover the sheet loosely with a large piece of parchment or foil to create a papillote effect.
Roast in the preheated oven for 55 minutes, or until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 75°C.
Remove the sheet from the oven and let the chicken rest for 10 minutes before carving.
Transfer the chicken and vegetables to a serving platter, spooning some of the pan juices over the top.
Garnish with fresh rosemary sprigs and serve immediately. Enjoy your low‑carb rustic dutch chicken roast!
